codeReview.summary: The fix removes the previously reported `.expect()` panic sites by privatizing Horizon variants and adding validated constructors, but a reachable panic path remains in `range_end()` due to unchecked integer overflow when rolling December to next year.

issues.description: `range_end()` computes `(*year + 1, 1)` for December months. `Horizon::new_month` and `Horizon::parse` accept any non-zero `i32` year (e.g., `2147483647-12`), so `*year + 1` can overflow and panic in debug builds. This leaves a panic path in the range methods and means invalid construction is not fully validated at construction time.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill requires manual verification including `cargo doc -p sd-core --no-deps`, but this fix handoff reports `followedProcedure: true` while only listing test/clippy/rg commands and no manual verification block.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/rust-library-worker/SKILL.md:68-75 (manual verification requirement, cargo doc); /Users/moritzbierling/.factory/missions/30fe7d21-09b3-49fb-8f42-d5d2ffc0b72b/handoffs/2026-03-08T03-48-38-714Z__fix-horizon-panic-paths__641a4bfe-d9cb-4cac-bc5f-b05f5a9c156a.json:13-21,75-76 (commandsRun only + followedProcedure true).

summary: FAIL: the fix resolves the original public-variant `.expect()` blocker, but `range_end()` still has a reachable panic path via year overflow (`i32::MAX` December), so panic-path safety is not fully achieved.
